questions.prompt: Confirm the acceptance record pins the reviewed plan version.
questions.guidance: Present acceptance.acceptedAt, acceptance.acceptedBy, and acceptance.acceptedVersion. The accepted version must match the current document version.

questions.prompt: Has the accepted plan been promoted to linkedPlanArtifact on the source idea?
questions.guidance: Acceptance is durable only when the plan-artifact version is pinned and the ideas row linked_plan_artifact pointer is updated per ADR.

questions.prompt: Is the plan ready for delivery handoff (task materialization)?
questions.guidance: Transition to delivered after execution linkage and delivery metadata are recorded.

plan.title: Phase Work Tree Diagram
plan.summary: Add a read-only dependency graph to the Overview tab that visualizes current-phase tasks color-coded by readiness state: blue (ready), yellow (blocked by in-phase tasks), red (terminally blocked by out-of-phase dependencies).

identity.title: Phase Work Tree Diagram
identity.summary: Add a read-only dependency graph to the Overview tab that visualizes current-phase tasks color-coded by readiness state: blue (ready), yellow (blocked by in-phase tasks), red (terminally blocked by out-of-phase dependencies).

goals: Operators can see at a glance which phase tasks are ready, blocked, or terminally blocked
Dependency relationships between tasks are rendered as a graph with directional edges
Color coding (blue/yellow/red) correctly reflects each task's readiness classification
nonGoals: Interactive nodes (clicking to navigate to task drawers) — read-only only in v1
Editing task dependencies from the diagram

userStories.iWant: to see a dependency graph of my current phase tasks on the Overview tab
userStories.soThat: I can quickly identify which tasks are ready to start and what is blocking progress
I can triage blocked work without reading each task's dependency list individually

valueAssessment.impact: High — reduces cognitive overhead when managing phase execution; blocked work is immediately visible without drilling into individual tasks
valueAssessment.rationale: Dependency data already exists in the task engine; the primary work is projection extension and rendering

riskAssessment.description: Graph layout may be poor for large phases with many tasks and complex dependencies
riskAssessment.mitigation: Use dagre hierarchical auto-layout; add a scrollable container in the webview

riskAssessment.description: dashboard-summary projection performance impact from dependency traversal on large phase task sets
riskAssessment.mitigation: Compute readiness classification in a single pass; cache per planning generation

riskAssessment.description: Adding a JS graph library increases the webview bundle size
riskAssessment.mitigation: Prefer a lightweight library (cytoscape ~200KB); lazy-load or tree-shake where possible

technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: New phaseTaskGraph field on dashboard-summary is additive; existing consumers unaffected
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: None — no task store schema changes

architecture.overview: Server side: extend dashboard-summary to compute a phaseTaskGraph payload with nodes (taskId, title, readinessState) and edges (dependsOn pairs). Client side: the webview Overview tab reads phaseTaskGraph from the dashboard message and renders it using cytoscape.js with dagre layout. Readiness classification is computed server-side using a single-pass dependency check against the current phase task set.

decisions.decision: Readiness classification is computed server-side in dashboard-summary, not client-side
decisions.rationale: Keeps classification logic colocated with task-engine data; avoids re-implementing dependency resolution in the webview

decisions.decision: Use cytoscape.js with dagre layout plugin for rendering
decisions.rationale: Operator requires high-quality visuals; Mermaid offers insufficient styling control for the three-state color UX
Explicitly confirmed by operator; interactive navigation deferred to a future iteration

uiUxDirection.summary: A new diagram section is added to the Overview tab. Nodes represent phase tasks with title labels; directed edges show dependency flow. Node fill color: blue (#3B82F6) = ready, yellow (#EAB308) = blocked by in-phase tasks, red (#EF4444) = terminally blocked by out-of-phase dependencies. A color legend appears near the diagram. Empty state message shown when no graph data is available.

criticalPaths: Readiness classification: correct three-state output for in-phase vs out-of-phase blocker detection
dashboard-summary phaseTaskGraph field shape and schema validation
Webview PhaseWorkTree component renders correct node colors for each readiness state
outOfScopeTesting: Visual regression / pixel-perfect screenshot tests in v1

implementationGuidance: Extend dashboard-summary projection with phaseTaskGraph: { nodes: [{id, title, readinessState}], edges: [{source, target}] }

Readiness algorithm (server-side): 'terminally-blocked' if any dependsOn id is absent from the current phase task set or points to a cancelled task; 'blocked' if all blockers are in-phase incomplete tasks; 'ready' if no incomplete blockers
Install cytoscape and cytoscape-dagre in the extension webview package; scope to Overview tab only
Wire PhaseWorkTree component to phaseTaskGraph field from dashboard-summary message; show empty-state when nodes array is empty
Define node color constants in a single theme file rather than inline per node
whatNotToDo: Do not compute readiness classification client-side — keep it in dashboard-summary server-side
Do not add click handlers or navigation to task drawers in this v1 task
Do not hard-code hex color values in multiple places — use named constants
Do not include the graph library in the shared webview bundle without scoping to the Overview tab
assumptions: dashboard-summary currently exposes the current phase task list with dependsOn arrays accessible to the projection layer
The Overview tab webview is a React component that can accept new fields from the dashboard message
cytoscape.js is acceptable to add as an extension dependency given the quality requirement

wbs.title: Extend dashboard-summary projection with phaseTaskGraph
goalMapping: Operators can see at a glance which phase tasks are ready, blocked, or terminally blocked
Dependency relationships between tasks are rendered as a graph with directional edges
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add phaseTaskGraph field to dashboard-summary projection
wbs.approach: Extend the dashboard-summary command projection to compute and return a phaseTaskGraph field. Implement the three-state readiness classification algorithm (ready / blocked / terminally-blocked) using a single pass over the current phase task set and their dependsOn arrays.
technicalScope: Type definitions for phaseTaskGraph node and edge shapes
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard-summary response includes phaseTaskGraph with nodes[] and edges[]
Each node has id, title, and readinessState (ready | blocked | terminally-blocked)
Readiness classification correctly identifies in-phase vs out-of-phase blockers
Unit tests cover all three readiness states and edge cases (no dependencies, tasks with no blockers, mixed in/out-of-phase)
testingVerification: Unit tests for readiness classification function using fixture task sets
Integration test: dashboard-summary response shape validates against expected schema
wbs.riskNotes: If dashboard-summary projection does not currently expose dependsOn arrays for phase tasks, that gap must be addressed first within this task
wbs.doneMeans: dashboard-summary returns phaseTaskGraph with correct node/edge data and all unit tests pass

generatedTaskPayload.title: Add phaseTaskGraph field to dashboard-summary projection
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Extend dashboard-summary command projection with phaseTaskGraph field and three-state readiness classification algorithm
technicalScope: Type definitions for phaseTaskGraph node and edge shapes
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard-summary response includes phaseTaskGraph.nodes[] and phaseTaskGraph.edges[]
Each node includes id, title, readinessState (ready | blocked | terminally-blocked)
Readiness classification correctly distinguishes in-phase vs out-of-phase blockers
Unit tests cover all three states and edge cases

wbs.title: Build PhaseWorkTree graph rendering component
goalMapping: Dependency relationships between tasks are rendered as a graph with directional edges
Color coding (blue/yellow/red) correctly reflects each task's readiness classification
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Build PhaseWorkTree component with cytoscape.js color-coded nodes
wbs.approach: Add cytoscape.js with the cytoscape-dagre layout plugin to the extension webview. Build a read-only PhaseWorkTree React component that accepts phaseTaskGraph data and renders a directed graph with color-coded nodes and a color legend.
acceptanceCriteria: PhaseWorkTree component renders a node for each task in phaseTaskGraph.nodes
Node colors: blue (#3B82F6) for ready, yellow (#EAB308) for blocked, red (#EF4444) for terminally-blocked
Directed edges render with arrowheads showing dependency flow
Dagre hierarchical layout applied automatically
Color legend rendered adjacent to the diagram
Empty state placeholder rendered when nodes array is empty
No click handlers or navigation wired to nodes
testingVerification: Component tests with fixture phaseTaskGraph data verify correct node count and color class assignment
Manual visual check against Overview tab with a real phase data set
wbs.riskNotes: cytoscape.js bundle size; verify tree-shaking or lazy-load path in the extension build before committing
wbs.doneMeans: Component renders correct color-coded graph from fixture phaseTaskGraph data with passing tests; legend and empty state work

generatedTaskPayload.title: Build PhaseWorkTree component with cytoscape.js color-coded nodes
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Add cytoscape.js + dagre layout to extension webview; build read-only PhaseWorkTree React component with blue/yellow/red node color coding and color legend
acceptanceCriteria: Renders all nodes from phaseTaskGraph with correct colors
Directed edges show dependency flow with arrowheads
Dagre layout applied; color legend shown; empty state handled
No click or navigation handlers

wbs.title: Integrate PhaseWorkTree into Overview tab
goalMapping: Operators can see at a glance which phase tasks are ready, blocked, or terminally blocked
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Wire PhaseWorkTree diagram into the Overview tab
wbs.approach: Add the PhaseWorkTree component to the Overview tab layout. Wire it to receive phaseTaskGraph from the dashboard message. Ensure graceful degradation when phaseTaskGraph is absent from the message (older kit versions).
acceptanceCriteria: PhaseWorkTree diagram section appears on the Overview tab
Diagram renders live data from phaseTaskGraph in the dashboard message
Tab renders without errors when phaseTaskGraph is absent or undefined
Section has a descriptive heading (e.g. 'Phase Work Tree')
testingVerification: Extension integration test: Overview tab with fixture dashboard message containing phaseTaskGraph renders the diagram section
Manual smoke test against live workspace with Phase 109 data
wbs.riskNotes: Minimal risk — primarily wiring; main concern is layout spacing with existing Overview tab content
wbs.doneMeans: Diagram visible on Overview tab with live phaseTaskGraph data, section heading shown, graceful empty/absent state handled

generatedTaskPayload.title: Wire PhaseWorkTree diagram into the Overview tab
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Integrate PhaseWorkTree component into Overview tab layout; wire to phaseTaskGraph dashboard message field; add section heading and graceful empty-state handling
acceptanceCriteria: Diagram renders on Overview tab from live phaseTaskGraph data
Section heading shown; graceful degradation when data absent

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 140
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Current active phase; feature is self-contained with no cross-phase dependencies. Fits within a single focused sprint.
